title: carum trachycarpum
intro: Carum trachycarpum, also known as wild dill, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is commonly found in the Himalayan regions of India, Nepal, and Bhutan, where it is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Carum trachycarpum can be prepared as an herbal tea, oil, or powder, and is often consumed in combination with other herbs to enhance its health benefits.
